Now let me compile this into an expanded framework with all the new options.","id":null,"provider_name":null,"provider_details":null,"part_kind":"text"},{"tool_name":"edit_file_llm","args":"{\"target_file\": \"/home/workspace/Research/career-fellowship/fellowship_framework_v2.md\", \"instructions\": \"Add a major new section after the existing content called \\\"## Expanded Options: 10 Additional Programs\\\" that includes the newly researched programs. Also add a section at the top about Berkman Klein timing.\", \"code_edit\": \"// ... existing code ...\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Berkman Klein Center: Timing Analysis\\n\\n**Current cycle (2025-26) deadline was December 5, 2025** — this has passed.\\n\\n**Next cycle prediction**: Based on historical patterns:\\n- BKC announces open calls ~October-November each year\\n- Deadlines typically fall in December-January\\n- **Expected 2026-27 cycle announcement**: October 2025\\n- **Expected deadline**: December 2025 or January 2026\\n\\n**Action**: Subscribe to BKC newsletter at cyber.harvard.edu/getinvolved for announcements. Start drafting your narrative now around \\\"AI & the Human Experience.\\\"\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Expanded Options: 10 Additional Programs\\n\\n### THINK TANKS & POLICY ORGANIZATIONS\\n\\n#### 1. GovAI (Centre for the Governance of AI) — Summer Fellowship ⭐ STRONG FIT\\n\\n| Factor | Assessment |\\n|--------|------------|\\n| **What it is** | 3-month AI governance research fellowship |\\n| **Location** | London or remote |\\n| **Duration** | 3 months (June-August 2026) |\\n| **Compensation** | Fully funded (stipend + housing + travel) |\\n| **Technical requirement** | Low — welcomes policy, comms, operations backgrounds |\\n| **Tracks** | Research Track OR Applied Track (non-research) |\\n| **Application deadline** | January 4, 2026 |\\n| **Part-time OK?** | ❌ Full-time required |\\n| **Your fit** | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ — Applied Track fits your profile well |\\n\\n**Why consider**: GovAI's Applied Track explicitly welcomes people from \\\"communications, policy, issue advocacy, events, research management, program management, operations.\\\" Your career coaching + founder background fits. Alumni have gone to RAND, IFP, Microsoft, Johns Hopkins.\\n\\n---\\n\\n#### 2. IAPS (Institute for AI Policy and Strategy) — AI Policy Fellowship ⭐ STRONG FIT\\n\\n| Factor | Assessment |\\n|--------|------------|\\n| **What it is** | 3-month AI policy fellowship with expert mentorship |\\n| **Location** | Washington, D.C. (2-week residency required) + remote |\\n| **Duration** | June 1 - August 21, 2026 |\\n| **Compensation** | $15,000-$22,000 stipend |\\n| **Technical requirement** | Low — diverse backgrounds welcome |\\n| **Application deadline** | February 2, 2026 |\\n| **Part-time OK?** | ❌ Full-time |\\n| **Your fit** | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ — policy angle on AI aligns with human amplification thesis |\\n\\n**Why consider**: IAPS is a nonpartisan think tank focused on AI policy. Fellows work on real projects (policy memos, briefings, research reports). Past fellows landed at RAND, Microsoft, government. D.C. network is valuable.\\n\\n---\\n\\n#### 3. AI Now Institute — Data Center Policy Fellow\\n\\n| Factor | Assessment |\\n|--------|------------|\\n| **What it is** | Research position at leading AI critical studies institute |\\n| **Location** | NYC (NYU affiliated) |\\n| **Duration** | 1 year |\\n| **Focus** | Critical perspectives on AI, labor, infrastructure, power |\\n| **Technical requirement** | Low — values diverse perspectives |\\n| **Your fit** | ⭐⭐⭐ — if your human amplification angle has a critical/ethical dimension |\\n\\n**Note**: AI Now has a specific perspective — more skeptical of AI hype, focused on power dynamics and labor. Worth considering if your vision includes ensuring equitable human amplification.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### COMPANY FELLOWSHIPS & RESIDENCIES\\n\\n#### 4. Anthropic AI Safety Fellowship ⚠️ TECHNICAL\\n\\n| Factor | Assessment |\\n|--------|------------|\\n| **What it is** | 4-month full-time research fellowship at Anthropic |\\n| **Location** | Remote-friendly (US, UK, Canada) with optional in-person |\\n| **Duration** | 4 months (May or July 2026 cohorts) |\\n| **Compensation** | $3,850/week (~$62K for 4 months) |\\n| **Technical requirement** | HIGH — empirical AI safety research |\\n| **Application deadline** | January 12, 2026 |\\n| **Your fit** | ⭐⭐ — technical bar is high, but >40% get full-time offers |\\n\\n**Reality check**: Anthropic wants people who can do empirical AI safety research. Would require significant technical upskilling. But the compensation and conversion rate are exceptional.\\n\\n---\\n\\n#### 5. OpenAI Residency ⚠️ TECHNICAL\\n\\n| Factor | Assessment |\\n|--------|------------|\\n| **What it is** | 6-month research residency at OpenAI |\\n| **Location** | San Francisco (in-person) |\\n| **Duration** | 6 months |\\n| **Compensation** | Competitive (comparable to Anthropic) |\\n| **Technical requirement** | HIGH — \\\"strong technical ability, research background\\\" |\\n| **Your fit** | ⭐⭐ — aspirational; would need significant preparation |\\n\\n**Note**: OpenAI Residency is designed to mentor people who \\\"have the potential to make outsized contributions to AI.\\\" Technical bar is real but not impossible for motivated career changers.\\n\\n---\\n\\n#### 6. Microsoft Research Fellowship — Human-AI Collaboration Track ⭐ INTERESTING FIT\\n\\n| Factor | Assessment |\\n|--------|------------|\\n| **What it is** | Academic collaboration with Microsoft Research |\\n| **Focus areas** | \\\"Human-AI Collaboration and Interaction\\\" is explicit theme |\\n| **Duration** | Project-based |\\n| **Who applies** | Faculty, PhD students, postdocs (you'd need academic affiliation) |\\n| **Your fit** | ⭐⭐⭐ — theme is perfect, but requires academic partner |\\n\\n**Your angle**: If you pursue a masters program (GA Tech, CMU), this could become accessible. The \\\"Human-AI Collaboration and Interaction\\\" theme is exactly your cognitive amplification interest.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### NON-PROFITS & FOUNDATIONS\\n\\n#### 7. Mozilla Foundation Fellowship ⭐ STRONG FIT\\n\\n| Factor | Assessment |\\n|--------|------------|\\n| **What it is** | 12-month fellowship for \\\"visionary leaders building better technology futures\\\" |\\n| **Location** | Global (remote-first) |\\n| **Duration** | 12 months starting May 2026 |\\n| **Compensation** | Funded (stipend varies by location) |\\n| **Focus** | Trustworthy AI, internet privacy, digital inclusion |\\n| **Technical requirement** | Low — welcomes technologists, researchers, creators, advocates |\\n| **Application** | Nomination-based — deadline January 30, 2026 |\\n| **Your fit** | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ — \\\"human-first tech\\\" aligns with your vision |\\n\\n**Why consider**: Mozilla explicitly wants people building \\\"people-first tech.\\\" Your human amplification thesis fits. It's nomination-based, so you need someone to nominate you — but your network should enable this.\\n\\n---\\n\\n#### 8. Schmidt Futures — HAVI (Humanities & AI Virtual Institute)\\n\\n| Factor | Assessment |\\n|--------|------------|\\n| **What it is** | Research grants for humanities + AI intersection |\\n| **Focus** | \\\"Insights from the humanities that will advance the development of AI\\\" |\\n| **Technical requirement** | Low — humanities perspective valued |\\n| **Your fit** | ⭐⭐⭐ — interesting if you frame career development as humanities discipline |\\n\\n**Note**: This is more of a research grant than fellowship, but Schmidt Futures is well-resourced and the humanities + AI angle is distinctive.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### GLOBAL PROGRAMS\\n\\n#### 9. Alan Turing Institute (UK) — Open Source AI Fellowship\\n\\n| Factor | Assessment |\\n|--------|------------|\\n| **What it is** | 12-month fellowship building open-source AI for UK government |\\n| **Location** | London (embedded in government) |\\n| **Duration** | 12 months |\\n| **Compensation** | ~£1M program (individual stipends competitive) |\\n| **Technical requirement** | HIGH — \\\"specialized AI model development, secure systems engineering\\\" |\\n| **Requirement** | Must be employed by UK organization |\\n| **Your fit** | ⭐⭐ — technical bar high; would need UK affiliation |\\n\\n---\\n\\n#### 10. Schwarzman Scholars — Tsinghua (China)\\n\\n| Factor | Assessment |\\n|--------|------------|\\n| **What it is** | 1-year Master's in Global Affairs at Tsinghua University, Beijing |\\n| **Focus** | Leadership development, China expertise, global affairs |\\n| **Duration** | 1 academic year |\\n| **Compensation** | Fully funded ($4,000/month stipend — highest globally) |\\n| **Technical requirement** | None — leadership + diverse backgrounds |\\n| **Application deadline** | September 2025 (for 2026-27 cohort) |\\n| **Your fit** | ⭐⭐⭐ — strong program, but not specifically AI/cognition focused |\\n\\n**Why consider**: If you're interested in the US-China AI landscape and leadership positioning, Schwarzman offers unparalleled access to Chinese institutions. The $4K/month stipend is the highest of any fellowship globally.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Summary: Employment-Compatible Programs\\n\\n| Program | Can Run Careerspan? | Domain Fit | Your Odds |\\n|---------|---------------------|------------|-----------|\\n| **GovAI (Applied Track)** | ❌ (3 mo full-time) | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | 50-60% |\\n| **IAPS Fellowship** | ❌ (3 mo full-time) | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | 50-60% |\\n| **Mozilla Fellowship** | ⚠️ (full-time but flexible) | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | 40-50% |\\n| **SPC Community** | ⚠️ (edge cases) | ⭐⭐⭐ | 70-80% |\\n| **Berkman Klein** | ⚠️ (hybrid possible) | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | 40-50% |\\n| **GA Tech OMSCS** | ✅ Yes | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | 80%+ |\\n\\n**Key insight**: Most prestigious fellowships require full-time commitment for 3-12 months. Your best \\\"run Careerspan in parallel\\\" options are:\\n1. **GA Tech OMSCS** (fully online, part-time)\\n2. **SPC Community Membership** (can make case for exception)\\n3. **Berkman Klein** (hybrid negotiable case-by-case)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Application Timeline (Key Deadlines)\\n\\n| Program | Deadline | Start Date |\\n|---------|----------|------------|\\n| **GovAI Summer 2026** | January 4, 2026 | June 2026 |\\n| **Anthropic Fellowship** | January 12, 2026 | May/July 2026 |\\n| **Mozilla Fellowship** | January 30, 2026 (nominations) | May 2026 |\\n| **IAPS Fellowship** | February 2, 2026 | June 2026 |\\n| **Schwarzman Scholars** | September 2025 | Fall 2026 |\\n| **Berkman Klein** | ~December 2025 (predicted) | Jan or Sept 2026 |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Recommended Portfolio Approach\\n\\n**Tier 1 (Primary Targets)**:\\n1. Berkman Klein Center (when opens)\\n2. SPC Community Membership\\n3. Mozilla Fellowship (get nominated)\\n\\n**Tier 2 (Strong Backups)**:\\n1. GovAI Applied Track\\n2. IAPS Fellowship\\n3. Afore FIR\\n\\n**Tier 3 (Skill-Building Parallel Track)**:\\n1. Georgia Tech OMSCS (HCI specialization)\\n\\n**Tier 4 (Aspirational/Future)**:\\n1. Astera Residency (after technical credibility)\\n2. Anthropic/OpenAI (after technical foundation)\\n\"}","tool_call_id":"toolu_01FWUwcGDPCwBW3UewfKrp5p","id":null,"provider_name":null,"provider_details":null,"part_kind":"tool-call"}],"usage":{"input_tokens":101325,"cache_write_tokens":3427,"cache_read_tokens":97897,"output_tokens":3150,"input_audio_tokens":0,"cache_audio_read_tokens":0,"output_audio_tokens":0,"details":{"cache_creation_input_tokens":3427,"cache_read_input_tokens":97897,"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":3150}},"model_name":"claude-opus-4-5-20251101","timestamp":"2026-02-07T13:27:21.140314Z","kind":"response","provider_name":"anthropic","provider_url":"https://api.anthropic.com","provider_details":{"finish_reason":"tool_use","zo_persona_id":"74e0a70d-398a-4337-bcab-3e5a3a9d805c","zo_persona_name":"Vibe Architect"},"provider_response_id":"msg_01Hy5TSM8XwLkzQkegTeRecT","finish_reason":"tool_call","run_id":"53b9131a-1338-4842-88c0-5c8d427c980b","metadata":null}
